title: CLAUDE.md
text: file provides guidance Claude Code (claude.ai/code) working code repository.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: DSIR small R package (“Data Science Infrastructure Global Health R”), targeted CRAN. provides: ggplot2 theme (theme_dsi) flextable defaults helper (dsi_flextable_defaults) pie-chart wrapper (ggpie) bundled dataset (wpro_cty, ISO3 codes Western Pacific Region) Thin API clients Global Health Observatory (gho_*) UN SDG (sdg_*) APIs package CRAN-bound, conventions driven R CMD check CRAN policy, just taste.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Run package root R session (via Rscript -e '...'): Spell-checked words live inst/WORDLIST; add new technical terms devtools::spell_check() flags .
code: # Regenerate NAMESPACE and man/*.Rd from roxygen2 comments. # ALWAYS run after editing any roxygen block or adding/removing an @export. devtools::document()  # Rebuild the installed package for interactive testing. devtools::load_all()       # fast, for iteration devtools::install()        # full install  # Run the test suite (testthat edition 3). devtools::test() testthat::test_file("tests/testthat/test-ggpie.R")   # single file  # Full R CMD check — must pass cleanly before any CRAN-related change. devtools::check()

what: roxygen2 is the source of truth
title: CLAUDE.md
text: NAMESPACE everything man/ generated. Never hand-edit . Exports come @export tags R/*.R; two importFrom(rlang, ...) lines driven @importFrom directives R/DSIR-package.R. function disappears NAMESPACE help page goes stale, fix almost always “re-run devtools::document()”.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: gho_*() sdg_*() hit live APIs. Per CRAN policy, must error remote unreachable. pattern (see .gho_get R/gho.R:153 .sdg_get R/sdg.R:171): httr2::request() |> req_retry(max_tries = 3) |> req_perform() wrapped tryCatch failure, cli::cli_warn() return NULL Public wrappers turn NULL empty data.frame() (NULL list endpoints) downstream code doesn’t crash adding new network call, follow pattern — stop() HTTP failure.

what: GHO uses OData; SDG uses query params
title: CLAUDE.md
text: .gho_get understands OData paging (@odata.nextLink) auto-follows exhausted. .sdg_get single-request helper; sdg_data layers manual page-number iteration top using body$totalPages. Keep split — don’t try unify . GHO filters built OData $filter strings URL-encoded utils::URLencode(..., reserved = TRUE). SDG filters plain query params joined &.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Examples hit network wrapped \dontrun{} current source (R/gho.R, R/sdg.R). NEWS.md claims \donttest{} — ’re preparing CRAN release, reconcile : CRAN reviewers can opt \donttest{} \dontrun{}, \donttest{} intended state.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: data/wpro_cty.rda binary form wpro_cty character vector; R/data.R holds roxygen doc block. DESCRIPTION sets LazyData: true, dataset available DSIR::wpro_cty without explicit data() call. update values, rebuild .rda (e.g. usethis::use_data(wpro_cty, overwrite = TRUE)) rather editing binary.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: theme_dsi() dsi_flextable_defaults() default base_family = "" / font_family = "" work CRAN’s Linux check machines Cambria isn’t installed. Don’t reintroduce hard-coded font default — break R CMD check non-Windows.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: ggpie uses .data[[.x]] / .data[[.y]] (rlang) reference columns string, R/DSIR-package.R imports rlang::.data. add another ggplot helper takes column-name strings, use pattern R CMD check doesn’t flag undefined globals.

title: DSIR
text: who_countries tibble lists 194 Member States ISO3C, ISO2C, UN M49 codes, official names, short names, region. Western Pacific countries, extra column is_pic identifies 14 Pacific Island Countries. convenience, DSIR offers pre-defined vectors ISO3C codes region. is_pic flag useful Pacific Island Countries often analysed group, given distinct demographic geographic profiles.
code: who_countries wpro_cty length(wpro_cty)   # 28 Member States in WPR (since May 2025) who_countries |>   filter(is_pic) |>   select(iso3, name_short)

title: DSIR
text: fetch indicators GHO, typical workflow three steps: search indicator code, fetch data, clean response. area argument accepts long ISO3 vector, whole region can pulled one call.

title: DSIR
text: Pick IndicatorCode result — value pass gho_data() next step.
code: gho_indicators("UHC") |> head()

title: DSIR
text: Note area accepts long ISO3 vectors — fetch 28 WPR countries one call.
code: uhc <- gho_data(   indicator    = "UHC_INDEX_REPORTED",   spatial_type = "country",   area         = wpro_cty,   year_from    = 2015 )  uhc |> glimpse()

title: DSIR
text: gho_clean() drops internal OData columns renames remains, leaving compact tibble indicator, location, year, three optional dimensions value / low / high.
code: uhc_clean <- gho_clean(uhc) uhc_clean

title: DSIR
text: theme_dsi() ggplot2 theme tuned -style charts — clean panels, modest grid, consistent accent color. Use drop-replacement theme_minimal() whenever chart heading deliverable.
code: uhc_clean |>   ggplot(aes(x = year, y = value, group = iso3)) +   geom_line(alpha = 0.6) +   theme_dsi() +   labs(     title    = "UHC Service Coverage Index, WPR Member States",     subtitle = "2015 onwards",     x = NULL, y = "SCI"   )

title: DSIR
text: dsi_flextable_defaults() sets -style defaults flextable globally — booktabs theme, bold headers, modest padding. Call near top report every subsequent flextable() picks formatting.
code: library(flextable) dsi_flextable_defaults()  uhc_clean |>   filter(year == max(year)) |>   left_join(who_countries, by = "iso3") |>   select(name_short, value) |>   arrange(desc(value)) |>   flextable() |>   set_caption("UHC SCI in WPR, latest year")

title: DSIR
text: sdg_data() sdg_clean() follow fetch--tidy pattern GHO counterparts. main differences indicator codes use dotted SDG format (e.g. "3.4.1") value, low high kept character — SDG API returns non-numeric entries ("<0.1", aggregate notes) rows, coerce .numeric() ready drop .
code: sdg <- sdg_data(   indicator = "3.4.1",   area      = wpro_cty ) sdg |> glimpse() sdg_clean(sdg)

title: DSIR
text: Source code lives https://github.com/shanlong-/DSIR. Bug reports, feature requests pull requests welcome — please file GitHub issue tracker. DSIR maintained Western Pacific Regional Office, Manila, shaped daily reporting work team.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Shanlong Ding. Author, maintainer.

code: @Manual{,   title = {DSIR: Data Science Infrastructure for Global Health},   author = {Shanlong Ding},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.5.0},   url = {https://github.com/shanlong-who/DSIR}, }

what: DSIR — Data Science Infrastructure for Global Health
title: DSIR — Data Science Infrastructure for Global Health
text: Data Science Infrastructure Global Health R package global-health data work. Bundles country metadata lightweight clients Global Health Observatory UN Sustainable Development Goals APIs, plus reusable -style themes ggplot2 flextable charts tables produced data look consistent across reports. Documentation: https://shanlong-.github.io/DSIR/

title: DSIR — Data Science Infrastructure for Global Health
text: current CRAN release (0.2.0) known issue gho_data() returns HTTP 400 error called full wpro_cty vector (28 countries) long region vectors. fixed GitHub development version (0.5.0). using DSIR regional analysis WPR / EUR / AFR / AMR countries, please install GitHub now: CRAN release 0.5.0 planned early June 2026.
code: remotes::install_github("shanlong-who/DSIR")

title: DSIR — Data Science Infrastructure for Global Health
code: # from CRAN install.packages("DSIR")  # or the development version from GitHub   # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("shanlong-who/DSIR")

title: DSIR — Data Science Infrastructure for Global Health
text: who_countries — tibble 194 Member States ISO3, ISO2, UN M49 codes, official short names, region, is_pic flag Pacific Island Countries. Regional ISO3 vectors — convenience character vectors region, derived who_countries:
code: library(DSIR) library(dplyr)  who_countries  # Filter Member States in the Western Pacific Region who_countries |>   filter(who_region == "WPR") |>   select(iso3, name_short, is_pic) wpro_cty   # 28 Western Pacific Member States (since May 2025) afro_cty   # 47 African Region Member States amro_cty   # 35 Region of the Americas Member States searo_cty  # 10 South-East Asia Region Member States euro_cty   # 53 European Region Member States emro_cty   # 21 Eastern Mediterranean Region Member States pic_cty    # 14 Pacific Island Country Member States (subset of WPR)

title: DSIR — Data Science Infrastructure for Global Health
text: theme_dsi() — publication-ready ggplot2 theme. dsi_flextable_defaults() — one-line setup flextable formatting (booktabs style, bold headers, paddings). ggpie() — quick pie charts automatic percentage labels.
code: library(ggplot2) library(dplyr)  # WHO Member States by region who_countries |>   count(who_region) |>   ggplot(aes(reorder(who_region, n), n)) +   geom_col(fill = "#0093D5") +   coord_flip() +   theme_dsi() +   labs(title = "WHO Member States by region", x = NULL, y = NULL) dsi_flextable_defaults() df <- data.frame(   region = c("AFR", "AMR", "EUR", "WPR", "SEAR", "EMR"),   countries = c(47, 35, 53, 28, 10, 21) ) ggpie(df, "region", "countries", .offset = 1.2)

title: DSIR — Data Science Infrastructure for Global Health
text: typical workflow search → fetch → clean. gho_clean() selects useful columns renames indicator, location, year, dim1–dim3, value, low, high. Output schema stable across indicators — missing columns filled NA.
code: # Search indicators by keyword gho_indicators("mortality")  # Discover available dimensions for an indicator gho_dimensions("NCDMORT3070") gho_dimensions("NCDMORT3070", dimension = "Dim1")  # Fetch country-level data gho_data("NCDMORT3070", spatial_type = "country")  # Fetch with area and year filters gho_data(   indicator = "WHOSIS_000001",    area      = wpro_cty,   year_from = 2015 )  # Tidy the raw GHO response raw <- gho_data("NCDMORT3070", spatial_type = "country", area = wpro_cty) gho_clean(raw)

title: DSIR — Data Science Infrastructure for Global Health
text: pattern GHO: search → fetch → clean. sdg_clean() renames SDG API columns snake_case (goal, target, indicator, series, location, location_name, year, value, low, high) flattens indicator list-column. value, low, high returned character preserve non-numeric entries ("<0.1", aggregate notes); coerce .numeric() downstream.
code: # Browse goals, targets, indicators, and geographic areas sdg_goals() sdg_targets() sdg_indicators() sdg_areas()  # Fetch indicator data sdg_data("3.2.1", area = "156", year_from = 2015, year_to = 2023)  # Tidy the SDG response raw <- sdg_data("3.2.1", area = "156") sdg_clean(raw)

title: DSIR — Data Science Infrastructure for Global Health
text: MIT — © 2026 Shanlong Ding

what: DSIR: Data Science Infrastructure for Global Health in R — DSIR-package
title: DSIR: Data Science Infrastructure for Global Health in R — DSIR-package
text: small, opinionated toolkit global health data analysis. bundles publication-ready ggplot2 theme (theme_dsi()), sensible flextable defaults (dsi_flextable_defaults()), quick pie chart helper (ggpie()), regional country-code datasets (wpro_cty), thin clients Global Health Observatory API (gho_indicators(), gho_data(), gho_dimensions()) UN Sustainable Development Goals API (sdg_goals(), sdg_targets(), sdg_indicators(), sdg_areas(), sdg_data()).

title: DSIR: Data Science Infrastructure for Global Health in R — DSIR-package
text: Maintainer: Shanlong Ding dings@.int

title: Set DSIR Flextable Defaults — dsi_flextable_defaults
text: Applies consistent set flextable formatting defaults publication-ready tables (booktabs theme, bold headers, modest padding). Pick font like — default "" leaves flextable default place call safe systems Cambria installed.

title: Set DSIR Flextable Defaults — dsi_flextable_defaults
code: dsi_flextable_defaults(   font_size = 12,   font_family = "",   font_color = "#333333",   border_color = "black",   padding = c(3, 3, 4, 4) )

title: Set DSIR Flextable Defaults — dsi_flextable_defaults
text: font_size Font size points. Default 12. font_family Font family name. Default "" keeps existing flextable default; try "Cambria" original DSIR look Windows. font_color Font color. Default "#333333". border_color Border color. Default "black". padding Numeric vector length 1 (applied sides) length 4 (top, bottom, left, right). Default c(3, 3, 4, 4).

title: Set DSIR Flextable Defaults — dsi_flextable_defaults
text: Invisibly returns NULL. Called side effect mutating flextable global defaults via flextable::set_flextable_defaults().

title: Set DSIR Flextable Defaults — dsi_flextable_defaults
code: dsi_flextable_defaults()

what: Create a Pie Chart with ggplot2 — ggpie
title: Create a Pie Chart with ggplot2 — ggpie
text: Builds pie chart data frame using one categorical column one numeric column. Slices labeled category name percentage share.

title: Create a Pie Chart with ggplot2 — ggpie
code: ggpie(   df,   .x,   .y,   .offset = 1,   .color = "white",   .legend = FALSE,   .label = TRUE,   .label_size = 3.5 )

title: Create a Pie Chart with ggplot2 — ggpie
text: df data frame. .x Column name (string) categorical variable used slices. .y Column name (string) numeric variable used slice values. .offset Numeric scalar (> 0). Controls label position along slice radius. Default 1 places label middle slice. Smaller values (e.g. 0.5) move label inward toward centre; larger values (e.g. 2) move outward toward edge beyond, useful donut-style layouts. .color Border color slices. Default "white". .legend Logical. Show legend? Default FALSE. .label Logical. Draw "name\n(pct%)" labels slices? Default TRUE. .label_size Label text size mm. Default 3.5.

title: Create a Pie Chart with ggplot2 — ggpie
text: ggplot object.

title: Create a Pie Chart with ggplot2 — ggpie
code: df <- data.frame(   category = c("A", "B", "C"),   value = c(40, 35, 25) ) ggpie(df, "category", "value")

what: Tidy a GHO Data Frame — gho_clean
title: Tidy a GHO Data Frame — gho_clean
text: Selects renames useful columns GHO observation table returned gho_data(), producing compact tibble suitable downstream analysis.

title: Tidy a GHO Data Frame — gho_clean
code: gho_clean(df)

title: Tidy a GHO Data Frame — gho_clean
text: df data frame returned gho_data().

title: Tidy a GHO Data Frame — gho_clean
text: tibble columns indicator, location, year, dim1, dim2, dim3, value, low, high, sorted location year. empty input returns empty tibble columns.

title: Tidy a GHO Data Frame — gho_clean
text: mapping : IndicatorCode -> indicator SpatialDim    -> location TimeDim       -> year Dim1, Dim2, Dim3 -> dim1, dim2, dim3 NumericValue  -> value Low, High   -> low, high Source columns absent df (example Low / High indicators without confidence intervals) filled NA, output always nine columns.

title: Tidy a GHO Data Frame — gho_clean
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ gho_data("NCDMORT3070", spatial_type = "country") |>   gho_clean() } # }

title: Fetch GHO Data — gho_data
text: Retrieves observations specific indicator GHO OData API, optional filters spatial level, country / region year range.

title: Fetch GHO Data — gho_data
code: gho_data(   indicator,   spatial_type = NULL,   area = NULL,   year_from = NULL,   year_to = NULL )

title: Fetch GHO Data — gho_data
text: indicator Character scalar. indicator code (e.g. "NCDMORT3070"). Use gho_indicators() find codes. spatial_type Character. Spatial dimension filter : one "country", "region", "global", NULL (levels, default). area Character vector country region codes (e.g. c("FRA", "DEU")). Default NULL returns areas. year_from Numeric. Start year filter (inclusive). Default NULL. year_to Numeric. End year filter (inclusive). Default NULL.

title: Fetch GHO Data — gho_data
text: tibble indicator observations, empty tibble service unreachable.

title: Fetch GHO Data — gho_data
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Country-level data for one indicator gho_data("NCDMORT3070", spatial_type = "country")  # Specific countries and years gho_data("WHOSIS_000001", area = c("FRA", "DEU"), year_from = 2015) } # }

what: List Dimensions of a GHO Indicator — gho_dimensions
title: List Dimensions of a GHO Indicator — gho_dimensions
text: Returns unique values given dimension across observations GHO indicator. Useful discovering ages, sexes, regions, breakdowns available calling gho_data().

title: List Dimensions of a GHO Indicator — gho_dimensions
code: gho_dimensions(indicator, dimension = "SpatialDimType")

title: List Dimensions of a GHO Indicator — gho_dimensions
text: indicator Character scalar. indicator code (e.g. "NCDMORT3070"). dimension Character. Name dimension column indicator data. Common values include "SpatialDim", "SpatialDimType", "TimeDim", "Dim1", "Dim2", "Dim3". Default "SpatialDimType".

title: List Dimensions of a GHO Indicator — gho_dimensions
text: character vector unique, sorted dimension values, empty character vector service unreachable dimension missing.

title: List Dimensions of a GHO Indicator — gho_dimensions
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ gho_dimensions("NCDMORT3070") gho_dimensions("NCDMORT3070", dimension = "Dim1") } # }

text: search Optional character string. supplied, indicators whose name contains search (case-insensitive) returned.

title: List GHO Indicators — gho_indicators
text: tibble columns IndicatorCode, IndicatorName Language. Returns empty tibble (message) service unreachable.

title: List GHO Indicators — gho_indicators
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # All indicators inds <- gho_indicators()  # Search by keyword gho_indicators("mortality") } # }

title: Pacific Island Country ISO3 codes — pic_cty
text: 14 PICs within Western Pacific Region, (pic_cty %% wpro_cty) TRUE. Non-Member Pacific areas (e.g. New Caledonia, French Polynesia, American Samoa, Tokelau) included. 14 PIC Member States : Cook Islands, Fiji, Kiribati, Marshall Islands, Micronesia (Federated States ), Nauru, Niue, Palau, Papua New Guinea, Samoa, Solomon Islands, Tonga, Tuvalu, Vanuatu.

title: Tidy an SDG Data Frame — sdg_clean
text: Selects renames useful columns SDG observation table returned sdg_data(), producing compact tibble suitable downstream analysis.

code: sdg_clean(df)

title: Tidy an SDG Data Frame — sdg_clean
text: df data frame returned sdg_data().

title: Tidy an SDG Data Frame — sdg_clean
text: tibble columns goal, target, indicator, series, location, location_name, year, value, low, high, sorted location year. empty input returns empty tibble columns.

title: Tidy an SDG Data Frame — sdg_clean
text: mapping : goal            -> goal target          -> target indicator       -> indicator (flattened first code source column list) series          -> series geoAreaCode     -> location geoAreaName     -> location_name timePeriodStart -> year value           -> value lowerBound      -> low upperBound      -> high Source columns absent df filled NA, output always ten columns. value, low high returned original character form SDG API returns non-numeric values (e.g. "<0.1" aggregate notes) rows; coerce .numeric() downstream appropriate.

title: Tidy an SDG Data Frame — sdg_clean
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ sdg_data("3.2.1", area = "156", year_from = 2015) |>   sdg_clean() } # }

title: Fetch SDG Data — sdg_data
text: Retrieves data one SDG indicators UN SDG API, optional filters area year.

title: Fetch SDG Data — sdg_data
code: sdg_data(   indicator,   area = NULL,   year_from = NULL,   year_to = NULL,   page_size = 1000L )

title: Fetch SDG Data — sdg_data
text: indicator Character vector indicator codes (e.g. "1.1.1"). Use sdg_indicators() find codes. area Character vector area codes (e.g. c("32", "76")). Use sdg_areas() find codes. Default NULL returns areas. year_from Numeric. Start year filter (inclusive). Default NULL. year_to Numeric. End year filter (inclusive). Default NULL. page_size Integer. Number records per page. Default 1000, maximum 10000.

title: Fetch SDG Data — sdg_data
text: tibble indicator observations, empty tibble service unreachable matching rows.

title: Fetch SDG Data — sdg_data
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # All data for indicator 1.1.1 sdg_data("1.1.1")  # Specific area and year range sdg_data("3.2.1", area = "156", year_from = 2015, year_to = 2023) } # }

what: DSIR ggplot2 theme — WHO publication-style — theme_dsi
title: DSIR ggplot2 theme — WHO publication-style — theme_dsi
text: clean, modern theme tuned global-health publications. Removes panel border, retains horizontal grid lines, uses muted text colour data — chart chrome — visual focus.

title: DSIR ggplot2 theme — WHO publication-style — theme_dsi
code: theme_dsi(   base_size = 12,   base_family = "",   accent = "#0093D5",   grid_color = "grey92",   legend_position = "bottom" )

title: DSIR ggplot2 theme — WHO publication-style — theme_dsi
text: base_size Base font size points. Default 12. base_family Base font family. Default "" (system default). Set "sans", "Arial", "Helvetica", "Calibri" specific look. Empty default keeps package portable CRAN's Linux check machines, Calibri unavailable. accent Accent colour used axis lines default highlight elements. Default "#0093D5" (blue). Pass colour string. grid_color Colour (horizontal) major grid. Default "grey92" — light grey, close bbplot OECD style. legend_position Position legend. Default "bottom". Pass "none", "top", "right", numeric vector c(x, y).

title: DSIR ggplot2 theme — WHO publication-style — theme_dsi
text: ggplot2 theme object.

title: DSIR ggplot2 theme — WHO publication-style — theme_dsi
code: library(ggplot2) ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, color = factor(cyl))) +   geom_point(size = 3) +   theme_dsi() +   labs(title = "Fuel efficiency by weight",        subtitle = "From the mtcars dataset",        x = "Weight (1000 lbs)", y = "Miles per gallon",        color = "Cylinders")

what: WHO Member States with regional and Pacific classifications — who_countries
title: WHO Member States with regional and Pacific classifications — who_countries
text: tibble 194 World Health Organization () Member States, standard country identifiers /Pacific groupings used across DSIR analytical workflows.

title: WHO Member States with regional and Pacific classifications — who_countries
code: who_countries

title: WHO Member States with regional and Pacific classifications — who_countries
text: tibble 194 rows 7 columns: iso3 ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 code (3-letter, e.g. "PHL"). iso2 ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code (2-letter, e.g. "PH"). m49_code UN M49 numeric code, stored 3-character string leading zeros present (e.g. "008" Albania). Stored character downstream APIs (notably UN SDG API) expect leading-zero form. name_official official English name (e.g. "Iran (Islamic Republic )"). Use formal documents reports. name_short shorter form suitable charts tables (e.g. "Iran"). Equal name_official countries whose official name already concise. See Details. who_region region code: one "AFR", "AMR", "SEAR", "EUR", "EMR", "WPR". is_pic Logical. TRUE 14 Pacific Island Country (PIC) Member States WPR, FALSE otherwise.

title: WHO Member States with regional and Pacific classifications — who_countries
text: official region listing: https://www..int/countries ISO 3166-1 codes UN M49 numeric codes: UN Statistics Division https://unstats.un.org/unsd/methodology/m49/

title: WHO Member States with regional and Pacific classifications — who_countries
text: Scope. 194 Member States plus 2 Associate Members (Puerto Rico, Tokelau) reports additional non-Member areas (e.g. West Bank Gaza Strip). dataset includes 194 Member States. Cook Islands Niue full Member States included even though UN member states. Region coverage (May 2025, EB156 reassignment Indonesia SEAR WPR): AFR (African Region): 47 AMR (Region Americas): 35 SEAR (South-East Asia Region): 10 EUR (European Region): 53 EMR (Eastern Mediterranean Region): 21 WPR (Western Pacific Region): 28 Short names. name_short differs name_official 13 countries official name parenthetical descriptor otherwise long. Examples: "DPR Korea", "DR Congo", "Lao PDR", "United Kingdom". short forms follow conventions used regional reports OECD Health Glance: Asia/Pacific. PICs. Pacific Island Countries flag (is_pic) marks 14 Member States Pacific sub-region: Cook Islands, Fiji, Kiribati, Marshall Islands, Micronesia (Federated States ), Nauru, Niue, Palau, Papua New Guinea, Samoa, Solomon Islands, Tonga, Tuvalu, Vanuatu. Non-Member Pacific areas (e.g. New Caledonia, French Polynesia, American Samoa) included dataset.

title: WHO Member States with regional and Pacific classifications — who_countries
code: # All WPR Member States, sorted alphabetically by short name wpr <- who_countries[who_countries$who_region == "WPR", ] wpr <- wpr[order(wpr$name_short), c("iso3", "name_short")] head(wpr) #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>   iso3  name_short        #>   <chr> <chr>             #> 1 AUS   Australia         #> 2 BRN   Brunei Darussalam #> 3 KHM   Cambodia          #> 4 CHN   China             #> 5 COK   Cook Islands      #> 6 FJI   Fiji               # Filter a data frame to PIC member states # df_pic <- subset(my_data, country_iso3 %in% who_countries$iso3[who_countries$is_pic])

what: WHO regional Member State ISO3 vectors — who_region_vectors
title: WHO regional Member State ISO3 vectors — who_region_vectors
text: Convenience character vectors ISO3 codes Member States region. vector regional subset who_countries's iso3 column, sorted alphabetically.

title: WHO regional Member State ISO3 vectors — who_region_vectors
code: afro_cty  amro_cty  searo_cty  euro_cty  emro_cty  wpro_cty

title: WHO regional Member State ISO3 vectors — who_region_vectors
text: Character vectors ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 codes. object class character length 47. object class character length 35. object class character length 10. object class character length 53. object class character length 21. object class character length 28.

title: WHO regional Member State ISO3 vectors — who_region_vectors
text: derived directly who_countries exist ergonomic filtering, e.g. dplyr::filter(data, iso3 %% wpro_cty). need update group membership, edit data-raw/who_countries.R re-run script — vectors regenerate master table. afro_cty 47 Member States African Region. amro_cty 35 Member States Region Americas. searo_cty 10 Member States South-East Asia Region. euro_cty 53 Member States European Region. emro_cty 21 Member States Eastern Mediterranean Region. wpro_cty 28 Member States Western Pacific Region. Includes Indonesia May 2025 (per EB156).

title: DSIR 0.5.0
text: theme_dsi(): removed color argument added accent instead. semantics different: color previously controlled text colour, accent now controls axis line tick colour. used theme_dsi(color = X) 0.2.x, change theme_dsi(accent = X) note visual effect shifted “everywhere” “axis ”. Text colour now fixed grey20 follow modern publication conventions.

title: DSIR 0.5.0
text: gho_data() now correctly handles long area vectors. Previously, passing ~22 ISO3 codes hit upstream query string length limit resulted HTTP 400 errors. Switched chained clauses OData operator, generates much shorter URL supports ~115 codes per call.

title: DSIR 0.5.0
text: New gho_clean(): post-processor gho_data() output. Selects useful columns renames snake_case (indicator, location, year, dim1-dim3, value, low, high). Output schema stable across indicators — missing columns filled NA. See ?gho_clean details. New sdg_clean(): counterpart sdg_data(). Renames SDG API columns snake_case flattens indicator list-column. Returns value, low, high character preserve non-numeric entries ("<0.1", aggregate notes); coerce .numeric() downstream. See ?sdg_clean details.

title: DSIR 0.5.0
text: theme_dsi() redesigned follow modern publication conventions (BBC, OECD, Financial Times style): Removed panel border; axis lines left bottom Removed vertical grid lines (kept horizontal ) Lighter grid colour (grey92, grey85) Larger title subtitle Text colour standardised grey20 Title left-aligned plot edge New legend_position argument gho_data() now infers spatial_type = "country" area provided without explicit spatial_type. Pass spatial_type explicitly silence message. gho_data() now validates area argument: must non-NA, non-empty character vector.

title: DSIR 0.4.0
text: New who_countries dataset: tibble 194 Member States ISO3, ISO2, UN M49 numeric code, official short names, region, is_pic flag 14 Pacific Island Country Member States. New regional ISO3 character vectors ergonomic filtering: afro_cty, amro_cty, searo_cty, euro_cty, emro_cty, plus pic_cty. derived who_countries stay consistent master table.

title: DSIR 0.4.0
text: wpro_cty now contains 28 countries (22 0.2.0). reflects May 2025 World Health Assembly decision (EB156) reassigning Indonesia South-East Asia Region Western Pacific Region, along Cook Islands Niue, full Member States previously omitted. Code aggregates wpro_cty (sums, counts, joins) produce different results DSIR 0.2.x — intended.

title: DSIR 0.4.0
text: Added data-raw/who_countries.R single source truth country-related datasets. Editing one file regenerates master tibble derived vectors.

title: DSIR 0.3.0
text: GHO SDG network functions now return tibbles instead plain data frames, improving printing wide result tables. Tibbles inherit data.frame, existing code continues work.

title: DSIR 0.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-04-21 Initial CRAN submission. Renamed DSI avoid name clash existing CRAN DSI package.

title: DSIR 0.2.0
text: gho_dimensions() returns unique values dimension column given GHO indicator, make easier discover breakdowns (sex, age, region, etc.) available calling gho_data(). sdg_targets() lists Sustainable Development Goal targets UN SDG API, complementing sdg_goals() sdg_indicators().

title: DSIR 0.2.0
text: theme_dsi() dsi_flextable_defaults() now default device / package default font (base_family = ""), work systems Cambria installed. Pass base_family = "Cambria" get original look. ggpie() gains input validation uses tidy-evaluation helpers internally, R CMD check longer reports undefined global variables. gho_*() sdg_*() now fail gracefully remote service unreachable, per CRAN policy packages hit network. warn return empty data frame (NULL) instead raising hard error. Full roxygen2 help pages every exported function, cross-references related functions. runnable examples use \donttest{} (\dontrun{}) network-dependent code, CRAN reviewers can opt running .
